# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
 name: fluentd-fsck  # namespace can also be used here, if you are using the same one for both services and deployments etc...
namespace: default  
---       
apiVersion: apps/v1   
kind: Deployment            
metadata:           
name: logging         
labels:          
app : fluentd-fsck              # assign a name that makes sense in your project  (e.g., 'fluentd')      
namespace: default       
specs:        
replicas:   1                     
selector:   matchLabels=app=logging            
template:          
metadata:           
annotations:     {"builtin-policy": "fsck"}      # you could add more policies here, like network policy etc.  (e.g., 'networkPolicy' => '' )             
labels:      
role : logging                      # name of the pods role                 ("fluentd" in this case)          
image:           <YOUR_IMAGE>     # change it according to your image     
volumeMounts:  
- mountPath:/var/log   
- mountPath:/var/lib/docker         
volumes:        
  - name: var-log                   
    hostPath:           /var/log       
  - name : var-lib-docker               # same as the above, for docker      
    hostPath : "/var/lib/docker"     
serviceAccountName: fluentd-fsck
